2013-02-01.title: What’s the Worst — and Best — Metro in the World?
2013-02-01.abstract: Prince Charles took the London underground to mark the 150th anniversary of the launch of subterranean travel in the British capital. Share your subway experiences with Rendezvous.

2013-02-01.title: As NATO Prepares for Afghan Withdrawal, Uzbekistan Seeks War’s Leftovers
2013-02-01.abstract: Uzbekistan wants to make a deal as the Western allies leave Afghanistan: it will provide the roads for shipping equipment out if the coalition leaves some of it behind.

2013-02-01.abstract: The fire was the latest in a series of house fires, most started by candles, in power-starved Gaza, and it set off bitter finger-pointing.

2013-02-01.abstract: The city government of Volgograd voted to officially restore its onetime Communist-era name, Stalingrad, for six days each year.

2013-02-02.title: German Legislators Vote to Outlaw Bestiality
2013-02-02.abstract: An amendment criminalizes “using an animal for personal sexual activities” and punishes offenders with fines of as much as $34,000.

2013-02-02.title: Another Reset With Russia in Obama’s Second Term
2013-02-02.abstract: Four years after Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. proposed a “reset” of relations with Russia, the United States is quietly adopting a new approach: the cold shoulder.

2013-02-02.abstract: Fighting that broke out last month between two Arab tribes in the Jebel Amir gold mining area has displaced around 100,000 people, United Nations officials said.

2013-02-02.abstract: Opposition to President Mohamed Morsi of Egypt led to violent clashes Friday outside the presidential palace in Cairo.

2013-02-03.title: Iceland, Fervent Prosecutor of Bankers, Sees Meager Returns
2013-02-03.abstract: In a country that has gone after financiers with far more vigor than others hit by the financial crisis, obtaining convictions has proved devilishly difficult.

2013-02-03.abstract: A Manhattan socialite, she started Bundles for Britain, which became an enormous World War II relief operation.

2013-02-03.abstract: With the number of self-immolations by Tibetans hovering at around 100, an eyewitness describes the large prayer meetings for the souls of the dead taking place in villages in Tibetan regions of China.

2013-02-03.abstract: An assault on a provincial police headquarters in Kirkuk by a suicide car bomber and other attackers left at least 35 people dead and dozens wounded, the police said.

2013-02-04.abstract: Zhu Ruifeng is a citizen reporter on a mission to expose corruption in China. But his passion for truth has cost him his marriage, and possibly more.

2013-02-04.title: Question of Aiding Cyprus Places Germany in a Bind
2013-02-04.abstract: As a Russian loan runs out and Cyprus seeks aid, Germany finds itself in a fierce debate over whether to help bail out the island nation.

2013-02-04.abstract: The administration is moving to approve the nation’s first rules for how the military can defend, or retaliate, against a major cyberattack.

2013-02-04.abstract: A growing number of young people are now seen as converting to be better socially integrated in poor neighborhoods where Islam is dominant.

2013-02-05.title: Egypt: Two Coptic Christians Sentenced for Stealing Weapons

2013-02-05.abstract: With more than 46,000 exotic animals seized from traffickers, vendors and trappers, the country’s wildlife centers are overwhelmed.

2013-02-06.abstract: The Obama administration has been more reluctant to reveal its own secrets than it was in revealing Bush administration policies.

‘Constant Drumbeat’
Sped the Pope’s Exit

2013-02-26.abstract: Clashes in the West Bank continued for a fifth day, and thousands attended the burial of a 30-year-old Palestinian who died in an Israeli jail over the weekend.

2013-02-26.title: Video Shows Group Holds Family Seized in Cameroon
2013-02-26.abstract: In the video, a hostage and a gunman say that the French family, taken last week on the Cameroon-Nigeria border, is being held by the Islamist group Boko Haram.

2013-02-26.title: China: 2 Tibetan Monks Carry Out Fatal Protests
2013-02-26.abstract: Two Tibetan monks have died in separate self-immolation protests in Tibetan regions of western China since Sunday, according to reports on Monday by two Tibet advocacy groups.

2013-02-26.title: Russia: Putin Signs Law to Ban Most Public Smoking
2013-02-26.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin has signed a law to ban smoking in most public places, the Kremlin announced Monday.

2013-02-26.title: Italy: Prosecutors Request Indictments for Cruise Ship Captain
2013-02-26.abstract: Italian prosecutors on Monday asked a judge to issue an indictment on manslaughter charges for the captain of the luxury liner Costa Concordia that ran aground in January 2012 off the Tuscan coast.

2013-02-27.title: Desperate Hunt for Day Care in Japan
2013-02-27.abstract: Japan subsidizes day care for families of varied incomes, but as more women work, they find themselves forced into an annual competition for coveted slots for their children.

2013-02-27.title: Afghan Sign of Progress Turns Out to Be Error
2013-02-27.abstract: The American-led coalition, which had said there was a 7 percent drop in attacks last year, said a clerical error meant the figure was wrong.

2013-02-27.title: Tunisia: Killer of Opposition Leader Remains at Large, Authorities Say
2013-02-27.abstract: The police have arrested four ultraconservative Islamists as suspected accomplices to the assassination of the leftist leader Chokri Belaid this month.

2013-02-27.title: Opposition Will Boycott Egypt’s Vote for Assembly
2013-02-27.abstract: The National Salvation Front objected that the governing Islamists had set rules for parliamentary elections without its input.

2013-02-27.title: No Move Yet by U.N. Body After Test by Koreans
2013-02-27.abstract: Diplomats said the process had bogged down mainly over bridging differences between China and the United States about how forcefully to respond.

2013-02-28.title: Germany: Gay Rights, Including for Marriage, Are Campaign Issue
2013-02-28.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel’s conservative Christian Democrats are struggling to catch up to changing attitudes among voters on issues like gay marriage and adoption.

2013-02-28.title: Switzerland: Shooting in Factory
2013-02-28.abstract: At least three people were killed and seven people were injured in a shooting at a Swiss factory on Wednesday, the police said, adding that the gunman was among the dead.

2013-02-28.title: North Koreans Blame U.S. for Fueling ‘Explosive Situation’
2013-02-28.abstract: North Korea’s representative to the United Nations in Geneva made vitriolic statements at the Conference on Disarmament in Geneva, capping a week of fiery rhetoric.

2013-02-28.title: U.S. Offers Training and Other Aid to Syrian Rebels
2013-02-28.abstract: The United States is helping to train rebels at a base in the region and for the first time offering armed groups nonlethal assistance and equipment that could help their campaign, said senior administration officials.

2013-02-28.title: As Castro Era Drifts to Close, a New Face Steps In at No. 2
2013-02-28.abstract: Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez, the top contender to succeed the Castros, will need to display authority while acting as if he does not want the presidency.
